rf.title: Radio Frequency (RF)
rf.description: RF-related regulations, testing, certification, and export topics.

regulations.title: Regulations
regulations.description: Overview of RF-related regulatory bodies and rules.

subsections.name: Radio Equipment Directive (RED)
subsections.summary: Key EU regulatory framework for radio equipment placed on the market.

entries.brief_summary: The Radio Equipment Directive 2014/53/EU applies to equipment that intentionally emits and/or receives radio waves for communication or radiodetermination.
Equipment must ensure health and safety, electromagnetic compatibility, and effective use of radio spectrum.
Examples include Wi-Fi devices, Bluetooth products, cellular modules, and other wireless transmitters or receivers.

subsections.name: ETSI Harmonized Standards
subsections.summary: Standards commonly used to demonstrate conformity under RED.

entries.brief_summary: Harmonized standards provide a presumption of conformity with relevant RED essential requirements.
Common examples include ETSI EN 300 328 for 2.4 GHz wideband transmission systems and ETSI EN 301 893 for 5 GHz RLAN equipment.

testing.title: Testing
testing.description: Overview of RF testing requirements and procedures.

subsections.name: Radio Frequency Testing
subsections.summary: Radio Frequency testing for UAV systems

entries.topic: Bluetooth modulation modes and channel plans used in RF evaluation
entries.brief_summary: ETC RF deck summarizes Bluetooth modulation modes relevant to RF evaluation: Bluetooth 2.1+EDR uses GFSK, pi/4-DQPSK, and 8-DPSK, while Bluetooth LE uses GFSK; the deck also shows BR/EDR and LE channel-list examples and 20 dB emission-bandwidth examples.

entries.topic: RED RF standards stack for common short-range and broadband wireless devices
entries.brief_summary: ETC RF deck maps the EU RED standards stack, including ETSI EN 300 220, EN 300 330, EN 300 440, EN 300 328, EN 301 893, EN 302 502, EN 301 357, and ERC Recommendation 70-03 for short-range, wideband, and 5 GHz systems.

entries.brief_summary: The low voltage directive (LVD) (2014/35/EU) ensures that electrical equipment within certain voltage limits provides a high level of protection for European citizens, and benefits fully from the single market.
